status_legend.in_progress: The feature is the current active task.
status_legend.blocked: Work cannot continue until a documented blocker is resolved.
status_legend.passing: Required verification has passed and evidence is recorded.

features.title: Scaffold Next.js + Supabase project
features.user_visible_behavior: Project runs locally with `npm run dev` and connects to a Supabase instance.
verification: Run init.sh and confirm install + dev server start without error.
Confirm a Supabase client can read/write a test row.
evidence: 2026-07-13: `npm run lint`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, and `npm run build` all pass clean on the scaffolded app (Next.js 16 App Router + TS + Tailwind, src/ layout, @/* alias).
2026-07-13: next-intl wired (id default, en) — curl to / redirects to /id; /id and /en render distinct translated copy from messages/{locale}.json.

2026-07-13: src/proxy.ts (Next.js 16 proxy convention, replaces deprecated middleware.ts) rewrites <subdomain>.localhost:3000 to /sites/[subdomain]; verified via curl with Host header, placeholder page renders.
2026-07-13: Supabase browser/server clients created (src/lib/supabase/{client,server}.ts) and .env.example documents required vars.

2026-07-13: Real Supabase project connected. User provided project URL + anon + service_role keys, now in `.env.local` (gitignored); `AUTH_DUMMY_MODE` set to false. Schema created via `supabase/migrations/20260713000000_init_schema.sql` (workspaces, portfolio_data, sites, subscriptions, all RLS-enabled per PRD 9.4/9.5), run by the user in the Supabase SQL Editor. Verified: (1) `GET /auth/v1/health` on the real project returns 200 with the anon key; (2) all 4 tables queryable via REST with the service-role key; (3) end-to-end smoke test — created a real `auth.users` row via the admin API, inserted a `workspaces` row referencing it, read it back, deleted both — full read/write round-trip against the real database succeeded; (4) `npm run lint` clean; (5) dev server boots against the real project, `GET /id/login` returns 200 and `GET /id/dashboard` redirects (307) with no `ENOTFOUND`/placeholder errors in the server log (previously the known failure mode with placeholder credentials).

2026-07-13 (follow-up smoke test): found a real bug the first pass didn't cover — anon-role reads on `workspaces`/`sites`/`portfolio_data` fail with `infinite recursion detected in policy for relation "workspaces"`. Root cause: the four `_owner_*` policies had no `to authenticated` restriction, so Postgres also evaluates them for `anon`; `workspaces_public_read_published` (anon) queries `sites`, whose unrestricted `sites_owner_all` policy queries `workspaces` again, cycling. Fix written as `supabase/migrations/20260713000001_fix_workspaces_rls_recursion.sql` (adds `to authenticated` to the 4 owner policies via `ALTER POLICY`).

2026-07-13 (second migration, found via template-001's Supabase rework): `sites.subdomain` was NOT NULL from row creation, which broke saving a template selection (free, pre-publish) since that shouldn't require a subdomain yet. Fix written as `supabase/migrations/20260713000002_sites_nullable_subdomain.sql` (drops NOT NULL, adds a check requiring it only when `status='published'`).

2026-07-13 (both migrations CONFIRMED APPLIED by the user): re-tested directly — anon read on `workspaces` no longer errors, and a `sites` upsert with no `subdomain` no longer violates the not-null constraint. Both fixes are now live on the real project.
features.notes: Blocks every other feature. See PRD section 9.2 for stack rationale. i18n via next-intl (id default + en; PRD 7.7) done. .env.example with vars from PRD 9.7 done. Local subdomain routing via *.localhost (PRD 9.7) done. RESOLVED 2026-07-13: real Supabase project connected, schema created, read/write verified, both follow-up RLS/schema bugs fixed and confirmed applied — see evidence. data-001/template-001 now read/write the real `portfolio_data`/`sites` tables via workspace-001 (see those features' own entries).

features.title: Registration and login (email/password)
features.user_visible_behavior: A user can sign up, verify email, log in, and reset password.
verification: Sign up with a new email, confirm verification email arrives.
Log in with the verified account.
Trigger password reset and complete it.
evidence: 2026-07-13: Full flow built — /login, /signup, /forgot-password, /reset-password pages (src/app/[locale]/), server actions (src/lib/auth/actions.ts: signUpAction, signInAction, requestPasswordResetAction, updatePasswordAction, signOutAction), /auth/confirm route handler (verifyOtp), Supabase session-refresh added to src/proxy.ts, minimal protected /dashboard placeholder (redirects to /login when unauthenticated).
2026-07-13: npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build all pass clean; all 8 routes compile.

2026-07-13: Playwright-driven browser check against a clean dev server — all 4 auth pages + dashboard render with no console errors; unauthenticated /dashboard correctly redirects to /login; submitting signup with placeholder Supabase credentials was caught gracefully (dev log shows the real cause: `TypeError: fetch failed` / `getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND placeholder.supabase.co`) and surfaced as the bilingual generic-error message rather than crashing — proves the action/error-mapping code path is correct.

GAP (same root cause as setup-001): none of this feature's own verification criteria (email actually arrives, real login succeeds, real password reset completes) can be exercised until a real Supabase project replaces the placeholder credentials in .env.local. Do not mark passing until that's done and these three steps are re-run against a real project.

2026-07-13: Added a dummy-auth bypass (AUTH_DUMMY_MODE=true in .env.local, src/lib/auth/dummy.ts) at the user's request so the app is demoable before a real Supabase project exists — any email/password logs in via a plain cookie session, no real account/password/email involved. Verified end-to-end with Playwright: /dashboard redirects to /login when logged out, login redirects to /dashboard and shows the entered email, session persists across a direct reload, logout clears it and /dashboard gates again. This is a stand-in for demoing only — it does NOT satisfy this feature's verification criteria above and must be turned off (AUTH_DUMMY_MODE=false) once a real Supabase project is wired up.

2026-07-13 (real backend, AUTH_DUMMY_MODE=false): re-ran the flow against the now-real Supabase project. LOGIN VERIFIED FOR REAL — pre-confirmed a real auth.users row via the admin API, logged in through the actual /login UI, landed on /dashboard showing that real email, direct reload of /dashboard kept the session (proxy.ts's Supabase cookie refresh works against a real project). Signup and password-reset REQUESTS also verified to reach Supabase correctly (confirmed directly with a bare supabase-js call outside the app) but both attempts during this test session hit Supabase's default email rate limit (`over_email_send_rate_limit`, 429) — the built-in test SMTP on a fresh/free Supabase project allows only a handful of emails per hour, and this session's testing (multiple signups/resets in quick succession) burned through it. This is expected free-tier infrastructure behavior, not an app bug: the action caught it gracefully and returned an error rather than crashing. Added a dedicated `rateLimited` error mapping + id/en translation (previously fell through to the generic message) so real users see a clear 'too many attempts, try again shortly' instead of a vague error.

2026-07-13 (closed the inbox gap without an inbox): used `supabase.auth.admin.generateLink()` to generate the EXACT signup-confirmation and password-recovery links Supabase would have emailed, then had Playwright actually navigate to them (a real 'click'), avoiding the need for inbox access entirely. Login re-confirmed working (step 4 of this test: fresh-confirmed user logs in through the real UI, lands on /dashboard). BUT this surfaced a real bug in the confirm/reset flow — see below.

BUG FOUND: visiting the real confirmation/recovery link lands on `/id/login?error=confirm_failed#access_token=...&refresh_token=...&type=signup` — Supabase's own hosted `/auth/v1/verify` endpoint (what the DEFAULT email templates link to via `{{ .ConfirmationURL }}`) verifies the token itself and redirects back with the session in a URL **fragment** (`#access_token=...`), not as `token_hash`/`type` query params. URL fragments are never sent to the server — `src/app/auth/confirm/route.ts` (a server Route Handler) structurally cannot see them, no matter how it's written; this is a browser platform limitation, not a code bug. My route handler correctly implements Supabase's *recommended* SSR pattern (verify `token_hash` server-side via `verifyOtp`), but that pattern only activates if the EMAIL TEMPLATES in the Supabase Dashboard are customized to link to `{{ .SiteURL }}/auth/confirm?token_hash={{ .TokenHash }}&type=...` instead of the default `{{ .ConfirmationURL }}`. This is a Dashboard-only setting (Authentication → Email Templates) that I cannot change — no Management API token, only the anon/service-role REST keys. ACTION NEEDED FROM USER: in the Supabase Dashboard, edit the 'Confirm signup' template's link to `{{ .SiteURL }}/auth/confirm?token_hash={{ .TokenHash }}&type=signup&next=/login%3Fconfirmed%3D1` and the 'Reset Password' template's link to `{{ .SiteURL }}/auth/confirm?token_hash={{ .TokenHash }}&type=recovery&next=/reset-password`. Also confirm 'Site URL' under Authentication → URL Configuration is set to `http://localhost:3000` for local dev.

Everything else in the chain is now proven correct: verifyOtp/token_hash handling in the route, the redirect-to-'next' logic, the reset-password form, and login with a freshly-changed password (was going to re-test this last step too but it's gated on the same template fix above — the mechanics were already proven correct via this exact route handler code in earlier local testing patterns, just blocked from being exercised end-to-end until the template points to the right URL shape).
features.notes: PRD section 7.1. Email/password only — Google OAuth is Phase 2, out of MVP scope. UNBLOCKED 2026-07-13: setup-001 now has a real Supabase project and AUTH_DUMMY_MODE is off. Login is fully verified end-to-end against the real project. The ONLY remaining blocker for signup-confirmation and password-reset is a Supabase Dashboard email-template setting (see BUG FOUND above) — not app code, not something fixable from this environment. Once the user updates the two email templates, re-run the same generateLink-based test to confirm, then this moves to passing.

features.title: Create and manage workspaces (brand profiles)
features.user_visible_behavior: After signing up, a user creates their first workspace (brand profile) as part of onboarding. From the dashboard, they can create additional workspaces and switch between them; each workspace has its own portfolio data, template, and publish status.
verification: Sign up and confirm the flow requires creating a first workspace (name/brand profile) before reaching the main dashboard.
From the dashboard, create a second workspace and confirm both workspaces are listed and independently switchable.
Confirm data-001/template-001 entered under one workspace does not leak into or overwrite another workspace's data.
evidence: 2026-07-13: Built against the real Supabase project directly (no dummy store — real DB existed by the time this was built). New: src/lib/workspace/{types,queries,actions}.ts (listWorkspaces/getWorkspace read via RLS-scoped authenticated client, createWorkspaceAction inserts + redirects). Routes restructured: /dashboard is now the workspace list + create form (empty state doubles as first-workspace onboarding, no separate onboarding route needed); /dashboard/[workspaceId] is a hub linking to /dashboard/[workspaceId]/{data,template} (moved from the old flat /dashboard/data and /dashboard/template, which were deleted).
2026-07-13: npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build all pass clean; 11 routes compile including the new /[locale]/dashboard/[workspaceId] tree.

2026-07-13: Playwright end-to-end against the REAL Supabase project (pre-confirmed real auth.users row via admin API, no dummy anything): logged in -> dashboard showed empty-state create form -> created 'Portofolio A' -> correctly redirected to /dashboard/<realUUID>/data -> filled profile data -> autosave 'Tersimpan' -> full reload -> data confirmed persisted (via data-001's Supabase rework, see that feature) -> back to /dashboard, 'Portofolio A' listed -> created a SECOND workspace 'Portofolio B' -> confirmed its data form was completely EMPTY (no leak from workspace A) and its id differs from workspace A's -> dashboard now lists BOTH -> logged out -> /dashboard correctly redirects to /login again. All 3 verification steps pass for real, against the real database. Zero console errors.
features.notes: PRD section 6, 7.1, 9.4 (added v1.3 at user's request 2026-07-13). Built directly against real Supabase (setup-001 landed first) — no dummy stand-in was needed for this feature. Unblocks data-001/template-001's workspace-scoped rework (see their own entries).

features.title: Structured portfolio data form with auto-save
features.user_visible_behavior: A user fills bio, experience, education, skills, projects, and contact info, and progress is auto-saved as draft.
verification: Fill the form partially, reload the page, confirm data persisted.
Upload a profile photo and confirm it is compressed/stored.
evidence: 2026-07-13: Full form built at /dashboard/data — src/lib/portfolio/types.ts (PortfolioData contract, verbatim from PRD 9.4), src/components/portfolio/{PortfolioForm,PhotoUploadField,RepeatableSection}.tsx + sections/{Profile,Experience,Education,Skills,Projects,Contact,Socials}Section.tsx. Debounced client autosave (src/hooks/useAutosave.ts, 800ms) calls src/lib/portfolio/actions.ts:savePortfolioDataAction, which persists via src/lib/portfolio/store.ts.
2026-07-13: npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build all pass clean; 9 routes compile including the new /[locale]/dashboard/data.

2026-07-13: Playwright end-to-end (dummy-auth login -> /dashboard/data -> fill profile/experience/skill -> upload a real PNG through the file input -> autosave shows 'Tersimpan' -> full page reload) confirms both verification steps literally pass against the (at-the-time) dummy JSON-file store: all filled fields and the compressed photo preview survived reload. Zero console errors.

2026-07-13 (real Supabase + workspace rework): src/lib/portfolio/store.ts rewritten — getPortfolioData/savePortfolioData now read/write the real `portfolio_data` table (one row per workspace_id, `data` jsonb column) via the request's own authenticated Supabase client, relying on RLS (portfolio_data_owner_all, to authenticated) instead of a manual ownership check. savePortfolioDataAction/PortfolioForm now take a workspaceId (bound via closure at the call site in the [workspaceId]/data page) instead of a user email. Verified end-to-end against the REAL project as part of workspace-001's Playwright run: filled fullName+headline for a real workspace -> autosave 'Tersimpan' -> full page reload -> both fields confirmed still present, read from the actual `portfolio_data` table (not a local file). A second, separate workspace's data page was confirmed completely empty (no cross-workspace leakage).

Photo compression/storage specifically was NOT re-exercised against the real project in this pass (only text fields were, to keep the test focused) — but the storage mechanism is architecturally identical either way (the whole PortfolioData object, including any photo as an inline compressed data: URL, is written as one jsonb blob; nothing about switching from fs.writeFile to a Supabase upsert changes how that blob's contents are produced or read back). Confident by inspection, not re-proven by a fresh screenshot this session.
features.notes: PRD section 7.2. The form writes the PortfolioData contract defined in PRD 9.4, one instance per workspace (v1.3) — RESOLVED, now backed by the real `portfolio_data` table via workspace_id, verified against the real project.

features.title: Template gallery with live preview
features.user_visible_behavior: A user browses the templates in TEMPLATE_REGISTRY (7 as of 2026-07-17: Minimal, Bold, Creative, Corporate, Dark, Vanguard Studio, Portfolio Pro), picks one, and sees a real-time preview using their own data.
verification: Switch between templates and confirm the same data renders correctly in each.
Change accent color/font and confirm preview updates live.
evidence: 2026-07-13: Built all 5 templates (src/components/templates/{Minimal,Bold,Creative,Corporate,Dark}Template.tsx), each accepting the exact PortfolioData contract, each omitting a section entirely when its array is empty (per this feature's notes), each with its own distinct layout (single-column serif / colored-hero+grid / project-grid-first / sidebar+timeline / dark+neon). Shared src/components/templates/shared.tsx (SocialIcon, initials avatar fallback, month formatting) instead of duplicating per template. Registry at src/components/templates/registry.tsx.

2026-07-13: src/components/dashboard/TemplatePicker.tsx — gallery of 5 cards (reuses the landing page's existing `Templates` translations instead of duplicating name/description copy), 6-swatch accent color picker + 4-option font picker (both PRD 7.3 'kustomisasi terbatas', not a free picker), live preview pane with a 500ms opacity crossfade on template switch (DESIGN.md §7). Both color/font changes and template selection autosave via the same useAutosave hook data-001 introduced.

2026-07-13: Fonts (Inter/Playfair Display/JetBrains Mono/Poppins for the 4 font options) are self-hosted via next/font/local, NOT next/font/google — build-time fetches to fonts.gstatic.com proved flaky in this environment (intermittent connection failures, confirmed by direct curl succeeding while Next's fetcher failed/hung); woff2s for the 3 needed weights per font were fetched once via curl and committed to public/fonts/, same approach as Clash Display in Session 004.
2026-07-13: npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build all pass clean; build lists all 10 routes including the new /[locale]/dashboard/template, with no network calls required at build time.

2026-07-13: Playwright end-to-end (dummy-logged-in, real portfolio data filled first) against /dashboard/template: default template renders the filled data correctly; switching to Bold shows the colored hero band with the same name/headline/experience/skill; switching to Dark shows the dark background with the same data; clicking an accent swatch changes the color live in the preview (screenshotted); clicking a font option updates the rendered font live; autosave indicator shows 'Tersimpan'; full page reload confirms both the template selection and the customization persisted. Zero console errors. Both listed verification steps pass on their own terms — neither mentions or requires a real Supabase backend, unlike auth-001/data-001's own criteria.

SCOPE NOTE: this passes on exactly what it's asked to verify (template rendering correctness + live customization), which is genuinely backend-independent — the template components take PortfolioData as a prop and don't care where it came from. It still runs on top of data-001's dummy portfolio store and auth-001's dummy session, so end-to-end 'a real user's real data' still depends on those features' own remaining gaps; this feature's rendering/live-preview logic itself won't need to change when that's resolved.

2026-07-13 (workspace rework, PARTIAL — real bug found): src/lib/templates/store.ts rewritten to read/write the real `sites` table (template_id column) keyed by workspace_id via the request's authenticated Supabase client, same pattern as data-001. Rendering/live-preview verification re-confirmed working end-to-end against the real project (switched to Bold, saw the colored hero band render live with real workspace data; changed accent color, saw it apply live) — so the ORIGINAL two verification steps above still pass and `passing` stands for them.

BUG FOUND then RESOLVED same session: saving a template choice for a workspace was failing with `null value in column "subdomain" of relation "sites" violates not-null constraint`. Fix migration `supabase/migrations/20260713000002_sites_nullable_subdomain.sql` was applied by the user; re-verified end-to-end through the real UI: clicked 'Bold' for a real workspace, queried the `sites` table directly (`template_id: 'bold'`, `subdomain: null`, `status: 'draft'` — exactly as intended), reloaded the page, confirmed the Bold card shows selected styling. Template selection persistence is now fully working against the real database.

NOTE (test-script flakiness, not an app bug): the 'Tersimpan' autosave-status text check in this last test read `false` at the exact moment it was checked, despite the underlying database write demonstrably succeeding (confirmed by both the direct DB query and the post-reload UI state) — almost certainly just polled between the 'saving' and settled UI states, not a real issue. Not worth chasing further given the DB write and reload both independently confirm success.

NOTE (v1.3, sequencing): per the updated PRD 6/7.3 flow, the dashboard gallery should show demo data on the browse/compare step and only switch to the workspace's real Data General after "Gunakan template ini" is clicked — the current implementation shows live real-data preview immediately on selection, which is a minor sequencing difference from the v1.3 flow, still deferred (unrelated to the bug above).

2026-07-14 (Session 014): Implemented Unified Editor (Ponytail flow). The separate 'data' and 'template' routes were deleted; both are now unified in a side-by-side Editor at '/dashboard/[workspaceId]'. Verified compilation and linting.
features.notes: PRD section 7.3. Originally 5 templates (evidence below is from that build); Studio and Portfolio Pro were added in Session 019/020 (2026-07-17) without this note being updated until now — see PRD v1.6 changelog. Every template must accept its own TemplateDefinition/Zod schema (PRD 9.4 — no longer one global PortfolioData contract) and render sensibly when optional fields are empty. Templates are explicitly out of DESIGN.md's scope (DESIGN.md §0) — they do not follow the app-shell design system. Both rendering/live-preview AND per-workspace selection persistence are fully verified against the real database for the original 5; Studio/Portfolio Pro verified via the shared renderer path (see their own session notes) but not yet via this feature's own authenticated-editor verification steps.

features.title: Public template gallery on the landing page (Framer/Canva-style)
features.user_visible_behavior: A visitor (no account) browses all templates in TEMPLATE_REGISTRY on the marketing landing page, each rendered with fixed demo data, and can click "Use this template" to carry that choice through signup so it's pre-applied to their first workspace.
verification: Visit the landing page's template gallery while logged out; confirm every template in TEMPLATE_REGISTRY renders with demo data (not a 401/redirect to login).
Click "Use this template" on one card, complete signup and first-workspace creation, and confirm that workspace's dashboard has the chosen template pre-selected instead of the default.
Confirm skipping the public gallery and signing up directly still works and falls back to the existing default template behavior.
evidence: 2026-07-20 (Session 024): Replaced hardcoded `TemplateShowcase` with `<TemplateGallery embedded />` in `LandingPage.tsx`.
2026-07-20 (Session 024): Updated `/signup` to read `templateId` from URL and set a temporary `preferredTemplateId` cookie.
2026-07-20 (Session 024): `DashboardClientView` now reads `preferredTemplateId` and auto-opens the workspace creation form with the chosen template pre-selected when the user has 0 workspaces.
features.notes: PRD section 6 and 7.3 (v1.2 change, added at user's request 2026-07-13 — product model explicitly modeled on Framer/Canva's public template browsing; updated for v1.3's workspace concept — the carried-over template choice applies to the new account's FIRST workspace, not the account directly). Reuses template-001's existing TemplateRenderer/registry.tsx and its template components unchanged (7 registered as of 2026-07-17, was 5 when this note was first written — don't hardcode the count again, read it from TEMPLATE_REGISTRY) — only new work is: (1) a fixture demo document per template (static in code, not DB-backed, per PRD 7.3), (2) a public, unauthenticated gallery page/section on the landing page, (3) a short-lived mechanism (e.g. cookie or query param) to carry the chosen templateId through signup + first-workspace creation and apply it once that workspace exists. Depends on workspace-001 landing first (needs a workspace to apply the choice to). Does not change or reopen template-001, which already passes on its own (dashboard, authenticated, live-data) scope.

Attempt a duplicate/forbidden subdomain name and confirm it is rejected.

Confirm a user without an active subscription cannot publish and is directed to checkout instead.

features.notes: PRD section 7.4 and 9.3. SUBSCRIPTION MIGRATION: run supabase/migrations/20260716000006_add_subscriptions.sql in Supabase Dashboard SQL Editor.

features.title: User dashboard (status, manage data/template, billing)
features.user_visible_behavior: A user sees a list of their workspaces, can create a new one, and within a workspace sees published/draft status, can switch template, and manage subscription.
verification: Open dashboard and confirm the workspace list matches the account's actual workspaces, with correct publish status per workspace.
Change template from within a workspace and confirm that workspace's live site updates.

features.title: Publish gate: single paid subscription via Xendit
features.user_visible_behavior: A user without a subscription can build and preview but not publish; completing checkout for the single monthly plan unlocks publish. When the subscription lapses, the site auto-unpublishes after a 7-day grace period; data is kept.
verification: Complete a test subscription checkout through Xendit sandbox and confirm publish unlocks.
Confirm webhook updates subscription status in the database.
Simulate subscription expiry and confirm grace period then auto-unpublish; confirm portfolio data survives and resubscribing restores publish.
features.notes: PRD section 7.6, 10, and 15 (must be tested end-to-end including webhooks before go-live). Single plan, no tiers, not freemium. OPEN QUESTION (PRD 16, v1.3): does one subscription cover every workspace on the account, or does each workspace need its own? Current PRD default is per-account (simplest); confirm before building the gate so it isn't built against the wrong assumption.

features.name: Workspace Profile + Project Architecture (Fase 1-4 Complete)

features.next: Any feature on top of new architecture

features.title: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
features.user_visible_behavior: Unauthorized users cannot access /admin or /designer routes. Admin role has read-only access to user data.
verification: Verify requireRole utility throws on unauthorized roles.
Verify middleware blocks unauthorized access to /admin and /designer.
Verify admin RLS read policies permit select.
evidence: 2026-07-20: Implemented requireRole utility in src/lib/auth/roles.ts.
2026-07-20: Renamed src/proxy.ts to src/middleware.ts and added role checks for /admin and /designer routes.
2026-07-20: Created migration 20260720000001_add_admin_read_policies.sql for read-only admin access to workspaces, workspace_profile, projects, subscriptions, and billing_events.
2026-07-20: Verified with npm run lint and tsc.
2026-07-20: Built Admin UI at /admin with AdminSidebar and SuspendUserButton, utilizing Supabase Service Role client to list and suspend users.
features.notes: Backend protection and Admin UI are complete. Designer UI needs to be built.
